channel.title: Posts on Ghassan Shahzad
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Ghassan Shahzad

item.title: Review - The Second Sons Trilogy
item.description: The World: The Second Sons trilogy takes place in a somewhat isolated corner of the world. We really only hear about three or four extant countries throughout the story. This fits because the story is less about conquest and more about intrigue (more about that later). The two main countries would be the island Kingdom of Dhevyn, and Senet. Before the story began, Senet had invaded and essentially vassalized Dhevyn. The first reason for this would be religious.

item.title: Notes - Predictably Irrational, by Dan Ariely
item.description: Full notes can be downloaded here Introduction We are not only irrational, but predictably irrational—that our irrationality happens the same way, again and again. Whether we are acting as consumers, business-people, or policymakers, understanding how we are predictably irrational provides a starting point for improving our decision-making and changing the way we live for the better. Dan Ariely tells us a story: When he was eighteen, he was the victim of an explosion of ‘a large magnesium flare’.
